title: Bebelplatz Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Bebelplatz Berlin is not just a pretty Unter den Linden square.** The most important part is the Empty Library memorial under the paving stones.
**The book burning happened here on 10 May 1933.** More than 20,000 books by banned authors were burned in public on the square then called Opernplatz.
**Look down before you look around.** The memorial is a glass panel with an underground room of empty shelves, symbolically sized for around 20,000 books.
**Give it 15 to 25 minutes if you can.** Read the memorial, the plaque and the surrounding institutions instead of treating it as a photo stop.
**Bebelplatz fits naturally into an Unter den Linden walk.** Use it between Museum Island, Humboldt University, Brandenburg Gate or Gendarmenmarkt.
**Nearest useful stations are Museumsinsel, Unter den Linden and Hausvogteiplatz.** The square is open, free and easy to add before or after a BerlinWalk route.
